name: technical-research
description: Technical spike and research investigation specialist. Use when exploring
capabilities: Structures timeboxed technical investigations with clear objectives and success criteria
Creates comparison matrices for evaluating multiple technical options
Documents proof of concept implementations with pros and cons
Identifies risks, dependencies, and mitigation strategies
Provides phased implementation roadmaps with effort estimates
Tracks open questions and unanswered items systematically
author: Joseph OBrien
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. This skill contains only markdown documentation templates with placeholder syntax ({{VARIABLE}}). The scanner misidentified template placeholders like {{CODE_SAMPLE}} as shell backticks, and words like 'description' as crypto/C2 keywords. No executable code, network access, or dangerous capabilities exist.
valueStatement: Technical decisions made without proper investigation lead to costly rework and technical debt. This skill provides structured templates for timeboxed spikes with clear comparison matrices and recommendations.
userTitle: Conduct Structured Technical Spikes and Research
limitations: Does not execute code or run automated tests
Does not fetch external documentation or APIs
Does not perform actual performance benchmarking
Requires manual effort to implement suggested POC code

useCases.title: Evaluate Architecture Patterns
useCases.description: Compare microservices, event-driven, and monolith approaches with structured POC code samples and weighted criteria matrices.

useCases.title: Assess Technology Feasibility
useCases.description: Determine viability of new frameworks or tools within defined time and resource constraints before committing.

useCases.title: Investigate Implementation Options
useCases.description: Explore solutions for complex features with documented trade-offs, risks, and clear justified recommendations.

promptTemplates.title: Evaluate New Framework
promptTemplates.prompt: Use Technical Research skill to evaluate [FRAMEWORK] for [USE_CASE]. Timebox: 4 hours. Answer: 1) Learning curve? 2) Performance impact? 3) Maintenance burden?

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Decision
promptTemplates.prompt: Conduct technical spike on [TOPIC]. Explore 3 options with POC code. Use criteria: performance, maintainability, team familiarity. Provide recommendation.

promptTemplates.title: Library Comparison
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare [LIBRARY_A] vs [LIBRARY_B] for [USE_CASE]. Create comparison matrix with: community support, documentation, bundle size, performance.

promptTemplates.title: Feasibility Study
promptTemplates.prompt: Investigate feasibility of [FEATURE]. Constraints: [CONSTRAINTS]. Document risks, dependencies, and provide implementation path if viable.

bestPractices: Define clear success criteria and questions before starting investigation
Stay within timebox and document what was not explored
Create runnable POC code rather than theoretical analysis only
antiPatterns: Starting spike without clear objectives or measurable criteria
Exploring only one option instead of comparing alternatives
Skipping risk assessment and implementation path sections

faq.question: What AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The structured templates guide any LLM through technical investigations.

faq.question: How long should a technical spike take?
faq.answer: Most spikes should be 2-8 hours. Longer investigations should be split into multiple focused spikes.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a prompt-only skill with no code execution or network access. Your data stays in your conversation.

faq.question: Can I adapt this for non-technical research?
faq.answer: Yes. The comparison matrix and structured approach work for any decision requiring multiple options analysis.

faq.question: What if my spike exceeds the timebox?
faq.answer: Document findings so far, list unanswered questions, and recommend a follow-up spike. Partial results are valuable.

faq.question: Do I need to write all the POC code myself?
faq.answer: The AI can generate POC code samples. You may need to test and refine them in your actual environment.
